messages.content: What is the boiling point of water?

chosen: 100 degrees C (212 degrees F) at sea level.
rejected: The boiling point of water is a very interesting topic in chemistry and physics. Water has the chemical formula H2O and boils at exactly 100 degrees Celsius, which equals 212 degrees Fahrenheit, when measured at sea level under standard atmospheric pressure of 101.325 kilopascals.
